Now `restoreStateFromSession` always reads the correct view layout. **Rule added:** Any code path that calls `applyColumnState` to apply a persisted view must also call `_saveViewStateToSession()` immediately after, before any async work. Never rely on `onFirstDataRendered` to leave the session in a consistent state — it fires asynchronously via `requestAnimationFrame` and can arrive after or before any HTTP response. --- ### 2026-05-27 — RFQ hard refresh (reset to defaults) did nothing when clicking sidenav **What happened:** Clicking "RFQ" in the sidenav while already on the RFQ page had no effect. The grid did not reset to its default state. **Root cause:** Angular's default `onSameUrlNavigation` is `'ignore'` — navigating to the current URL is a no-op. Without component recreation, no grid reset could happen. **Recovery:** (`6cb2dd82`, `17c89da5`) Two changes: 1. Added `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` to `RouterModule.forRoot` in `app-routing.module.ts` so same-URL navigation recreates the component. 2. Modified `sidenav-item.component.ts` `onClick()` to set `sessionStorage['rfq_hard_reset_pending'] = '1'` when the destination URL equals the current URL. `restoreStateFromSession()` reads and clears this flag — when set, it removes `gridState_rfq` from sessionStorage and returns early, letting the component load fresh from the API. **Rule added:** To trigger a component hard-reset via navigation, both pieces are required: `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` (so the router acts) AND a sessionStorage flag (so `restoreStateFromSession` does not immediately re-apply the stale snapshot). The "Reset View" button approach was rejected in favor of using the sidenav click to keep the toolbar clean. --- ### 2026-05-27 — Step column cell editor slow on first click (duplicate API call + race condition) **What happened:** Clicking into a Step cell for the first time took several seconds to show the 6 options, even though `loadRFQSteps()` already fetched the step data on component init. **Root cause (slowness):** `loadRFQSteps()` populated `this.stepList` but did NOT write to `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step`. So `rfq-autocomplete.component.ts` always saw the cache empty and made a fresh `getRFQStep()` API call on every first-click. **Root cause (race condition):** The cache guard was `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step)`. When a second editor opened while the first was mid-fetch, it saw the truthy `{}` placeholder set before the fetch completed, went to the `else` branch, and read `undefined` from `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step.options` — resulting in an empty dropdown. **Recovery:** (`6cb2dd82`) 1. `loadRFQSteps()` now writes `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step = { options: data.map(...) }` after the fetch. First click is instant. 2. Autocomplete guard changed from `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step)` to `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step?.options)` — truthy `{}` is no longer treated as a populated cache. **Rule added:** When pre-populating `window['rfq-autocomplete']` caches, always set the full shape including the `options` key in one assignment, never set an empty object first. Guard checks must test for the presence of `options` specifically, not just the parent object, to be safe against in-flight race conditions. --- ### 2026-05-27 — Smart-quote inch mark left `x12mm` artifact in cleaned description **What happened:** User reported "Classifying: x12mm Anti-fatigue PVC Kitchen Mat" — the CROSS search ran with `x12mm` as part of the search term and returned zero results. **Root cause:** The raw product description contained a curly/Unicode inch mark `"` (U+201C) in a dimension spec like `18"×12mm`. The dimension pre-pass regex `['"]` matches only ASCII — the Unicode `"` slipped through. Step 7 (miscellaneous char strip) converted the curly `"` to a space, producing `18 x12mm`. Step 7b stripped the bare standalone `18`, leaving `x12mm` as the entire cleaned description. **Fix:** Added Step 0 to `desc-cleaner.ts`: normalize all Unicode smart/curly quote and prime variants to their ASCII equivalents (`"` and `'`) before any dimension regex runs. Also added `\bx\d+(\.\d+)?\s*(unit)?\b` artifact cleanup as a belt-and-suspenders fallback for the `x` prefix pattern. **Rule added:** Always normalize smart quotes to ASCII before regex dimension stripping. The artifact cleanup regex catches leftovers but the root fix is normalization in Step 0. --- ### 2026-05-27 — AI-driven CROSS retry replaced naive word-drop **What happened:** Initial implementation of CROSS no-results retry dropped leading words one at a time ("Anti-fatigue PVC Kitchen Mat" → "PVC Kitchen Mat" → "Kitchen Mat"). User rejected this: "why drop leading words blindly? who's to say the leading words are the extraneous ones?" **Root cause:** Leading words are NOT always the least relevant to classification. "PVC Kitchen Mat" — "PVC" is the first word and the material (the most important HTS classification signal). Naive leading-word-drop would produce "Kitchen Mat" — too generic and wrong priority. **Fix:** `suggestCrossTerms()` — a cheap no-reasoning AI call that returns 3–5 suggested search terms. The AI identifies which words are classification-relevant (material, product type) vs. incidental (qualifiers, attributes). It also suggests alternate spellings CBP rulings commonly use (hyphenated → unhyphenated: "anti-fatigue" → "antifatigue", "anti fatigue"). **Rule added:** Never use positional heuristics (drop leading/trailing words) to trim search terms. Classification-relevant words can appear anywhere in a description. Use AI-driven selection, which understands semantic relevance. --- ### 2026-05-31 — RFQ detail-grid price sort shipped as a silent no-op (wrong colId) **What happened:** The new "default-sort the vendor-quotes detail grid by price, lowest first" feature was committed and pushed (`e8ba0551`), but on screen nothing changed — the `Name` column still owned the sort and prices were unordered. **Root cause:** The sort (`sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator`) was set only on the column with colId `quote_price`. The detail grid loads its columns from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), and the live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under the **legacy colId `price`**, not `quote_price`. The sort landed on a non-existent column, so AG Grid applied no price sort and `Name`'s `colDef.sort` kept winning. **Recovery:** (`b241ce79`) Extracted `applyDefaultPriceSort()`, which targets whichever price colId the layout provides (`quote_price` or `price`, preferring the visible one), and made the comparator read `node.data.Price` directly so it is independent of colId/field/valueGetter. Verified with unit tests covering both colIds and the unpriced/declined bottom-sink rule. **Rule added:** The detail grid's columns are backend-driven (`GridLayout`), so do not assume their `colId`s match the in-repo `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts`. When wiring behavior onto a detail-grid column by colId, match against the colIds the running layout actually serves (currently `price` for the price column), and prefer reading row data over the cell value in comparators. See §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one". --- ## 15. ## Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (2026-06-05)

A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.

### What it does

Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:

1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)

On the backend, the import creates:
- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

over description fields in `data/hts-rates.json`. Inject the top 5 matches into the AI's

# Architecture

## System overview

Designflow Frontend is a pure Angular SPA — no server-side rendering. It communicates with a separate backend REST API over HTTPS. The frontend is responsible for all UI, routing, role enforcement, and business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed cost). The backend handles persistence and data retrieval.

```
Browser
  └── Angular SPA (this repo)
        ├── AG Grid Enterprise (primary data display and editing)
        ├── Angular Material (forms, dialogs, snackbars)
        ├── Auth (JWT in localStorage, idle timeout)
        └── HTTP client
              └── Backend REST API (separate service)
                    ├── /api/core        ← main CRUD
                    ├── /api/tracking    ← production/licensing tracking
                    ├── /api/item_master ← item library
                    └── /api/data_sync   ← sync operations
```

---

## Module structure

```
src/app/
├── app.module.ts              Root NgModule (providers, interceptors, AG Grid registration)
├── app-routing.module.ts      All route definitions and guards
├── app.component.ts           Root component: nav menu, query-param theme switching
├── custom-layout/             Shell layout wrapping all authenticated routes
├── pages/                     Feature modules (see below)
├── helpers/
│   ├── services/              API services (main.service.ts is the primary one)
│   ├── models/                TypeScript interfaces
│   ├── interceptor/           HTTP interceptors (auth, loader, error)
│   └── ag-grid/               AG Grid column definitions per feature
└── components/                Shared reusable components (global loader, file upload, etc.)
```

---

## Feature modules

### RFQ (`src/app/pages/rfq/`)

The primary feature module. Manages vendor quotes for product sourcing.

**Key components:**
- `rfq.component.ts` — master grid, column wiring, valueSetter orchestration (~3600 lines)
- `detail-cell-renderer/` — detail row renderer for vendor quotes (AG Grid master-detail)
- `helpers/equation.ts` — all margin and sell-price calculations
- `helpers/api-error.ts` — shared HTTP error message extraction

**Column configuration:** `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`

**Dialog sub-components:** `addNewProduct/`, `duplicate_dialog/`, `container_cost_dialog/`, `duty_rate_dialog/`, `multi_quote/`

See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the RFQ calculation pipeline and AG Grid patterns.

### Item Library (`src/app/pages/itemLibrary/`)

Product master data management. Single AG Grid with custom column definitions from `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/item.library.config.ts`.

### Production Tracking (`src/app/pages/prod_tracking/`)

Tracks production stages per item. Has its own `equation.ts` for production-specific calculations. Uses master-detail AG Grid (same pattern as RFQ).

### Licensing Tracking (`src/app/pages/licensing_tracking/`)

Tracks licensing items and royalty calculations. Has its own `equation.ts`.

### Sample Tracking (`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/`)

Tracks physical product samples moving between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices
(request / factory-offer / USA-bought origins; bidirectional check-in/out). A reskin over the
`designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API using the same building blocks as the other
tracking grids: master grid with a master-detail event timeline, plus dialogs for create,
check-in/out, sample detail (Info/Photos/Comments/Timeline), and box/group management. Photos
upload to DigitalOcean Spaces via a presigned-URL flow (`fetch` PUT bypasses the HTTP
interceptors, matching the existing pattern in `helper.service.ts`). The status state machine is
mirrored client-side in `helpers/sample.status.ts` for labels and next-status preview, but the
server is authoritative (`POST /sample/events`). Samples live in their own backend tables (not
`itemHeader`) and link back to licensing/production via `item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk`.
User-facing labels avoid raw "inbound/outbound": a sample's direction shows as a **Journey**
(`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory`) and boxes are created/shown by **route**
(`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`); the grid has a **Box** column (from the list endpoint's
derived `box_label`). To box samples: tick rows in the grid → **Boxes & Groups** → **Add selected**.

### Standardized Products (`src/app/pages/standardized/`)

Manages standardized/baseline product definitions used as templates for new RFQ items.

### Admin / Editor (`src/app/pages/editor/`)

Role-restricted to `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`. Provides grid-level configuration editing.

---

## Authentication and authorization

Two login paths both produce the same Designflow RS256 JWT and follow the same post-login flow:

**Password login:**
1. User submits email + password → `POST /auth/login` → backend returns JWT
2. `auth.service.ts` stores token in `localStorage`

**Microsoft SSO (Azure AD):**
1. `AzureSsoService.initialize()` runs at app start via `APP_INITIALIZER` — creates the MSAL `PublicClientApplication` instance (MSAL Browser 5.x)
2. User clicks "Sign in with Microsoft" → `AzureSsoService.loginPopup()` opens a Microsoft popup with scopes `['openid', 'profile', 'email']`; the popup redirects to `/assets/auth-redirect.html` (the MSAL redirect bridge page), which fires a `BroadcastChannel` message back to the parent window and closes
3. MSAL returns the Azure AD ID token → `POST /auth/azure` → backend verifies the token via Azure JWKS, looks up or auto-provisions the user, returns a Designflow JWT
4. `auth.service.ts` stores the returned Designflow JWT in `localStorage`

**Common post-login flow:**
1. `UserAuthInterceptor` injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every outgoing request
2. `AuthGuardService.canActivate()` calls `auth.service.isAuthenticated()` → verifies token with backend + checks JWT expiry
3. `RoleGuardService.canActivate()` first verifies authentication, then decodes the JWT payload and compares `role` to `route.data.role`
4. `IdleTimeoutService` watches for inactivity and logs the user out

**Role values (from JWT payload):** `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`

**Auto-provisioning:** First-time Azure SSO users are created in the `users` table with `level: 'designer'`. An admin promotes them via User Management.

### Route Role Enforcement

What changed:
Admin/section routes that declare `data.role` use `RoleGuardService`, not login-only `AuthGuardService`. User Management is explicitly `admin` only, and direct Art Piece detail URLs use the same role set as the Art Piece list/archive routes.

Why:
The navigation menu hid many pages by role, but direct URLs still loaded because several route definitions had `data.role` beside `AuthGuardService`. `AuthGuardService` only proves the user is logged in; it does not read `route.data.role`.

Future sessions should:
When adding a route with `data.role`, wire `canActivate: [RoleGuard]`, keep the sidenav role in `app.component.ts` aligned, and update `src/app/app-routing.module.spec.ts` if the route is part of a protected section.

**Single-use token:** `/singleUseToken/:token/:rfqItem_priceCode/:highlight` — allows vendors to access a specific RFQ item without a full account.

**Token storage:** `localStorage` (not a cookie). Tokens survive page refresh and are cleared on `localStorage.clear()`.

**MSAL redirect bridge:** MSAL Browser 5.x changed the popup flow to require a dedicated blank page at the redirect URI instead of the full SPA. `/assets/auth-redirect.html` loads `msal-redirect-bridge.min.js` (copied from `node_modules/@azure/msal-browser/lib/redirect-bridge` at build time) and calls `broadcastResponseToMainFrame()`. Without this page, the popup would load the full Angular app and never relay the auth code back to the parent window.

---

## HTTP interceptor stack

Interceptors run in declaration order (from `app.module.ts`):

1. **`UserAuthInterceptor`** — attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header from localStorage
2. **`LoaderInterceptor`** — shows/hides global spinner for requests that include `X-Enable-Loader: true`; call `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` instead of `this.setUserAuth()` in `main.service.ts` to opt in
3. **`HttpErrorInterceptor`** — catches HTTP errors, extracts a user-facing message via `src/app/helpers/api-error.ts`, displays it in a snackbar

---

## AG Grid patterns

All feature grids use AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1 with the client-side row model (CSRM).

### Column identity

Each column has two identifiers:
- **`field`** — the key used to read/write `params.data[field]`. Can contain spaces and special characters (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`).
- **`colId`** — the stable lowercase-underscore identifier used in code (e.g. `"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`).

`getMasterFiledName(colId)` (in both `rfq.component.ts` and `equation.ts`) resolves a colId to its field name by iterating `this.colHeaders`. **Always use this function** — never hardcode a field name string in calculation code.

### Master-detail grids

RFQ and Production Tracking use AG Grid master-detail. The master row is an RFQ item; the detail row is vendor quotes. The `DetailCellRenderer` component manages the detail grid independently, with its own column definitions loaded from the backend.

### Row grouping

The RFQ grid groups by `RFQ Group` (column with `rowGroup: true`, `enableRowGroup: true`). Group rows do not have `params.data` in AG Grid's aggregation pipeline. **Do not add `aggFunc` to any column** — see AGENTS.md §11 for the full explanation of why.

### `postSortRows`

Used on the RFQ grid to keep group rows ordered by Date Modified without activating the aggregation pipeline.

### Async valueSetters

AG Grid v35 awaits an `async valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise cancels the edit and reverts the cell. Every async valueSetter must:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` (or call `_snackBar.open(...)`) on validation failure
- Never let an uncaught exception propagate (wrap in try-catch)

### View layout persistence

The RFQ grid supports named views (column visibility, filter state, column order) saved per user via `POST /saveGridLayout` / `POST /getCustomGridLayout`. The `layout-dropdown` component manages this.

---

## RFQ calculation pipeline

See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the full formula reference.

**Summary:** When a user edits a sourcing field (FOB Cost, CBM/pc, agent commission, duty rate, freight, container cost), the valueSetter chain is:

```
User edits cell
  → valueSetter in rfq.component.ts
      → calculateLandedCost() (if relevant field changed)
          → updates landed_cost field on params.data
      → equation.recalculate_Lic_Gen(params, colHeaders)
          → setGen{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
          → setLic{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
              → each setter computes sell price from margin, or margin from sell price
  → return true (commit the edit)
```

---

## Data flow: loading the RFQ grid

```
ngOnInit
  → getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq' })    ← column definitions from backend
      → applyAutoFormatters()             ← apply date formatters, comparators
      → applyColumnDefinitions()          ← wire valueSetters, valueGetters per colId
  → getAllItem({ status, divisionCode })  ← row data
      → gridApi.setRowData()
  → getCustomGridLayout()                 ← restore saved column/filter state
```

---

## Vex layout framework (`src/@vex/`)

The Vex commercial template provides:
- Sidenav layout with configurable colors and modes
- Navigation service (programmatic menu definition in `app.component.ts`)
- Config service (layout settings, theme, panel behavior)
- Reusable animations, directives, and utility pipes

**Our code in `app.component.ts` configures the navigation items programmatically**, applying role-based `hidden` flags per menu item. The Vex framework reads these at render time.

The `@vex/` directory is committed into the repo (not installed as a package). Treat it as upstream — avoid modifications unless specifically needed.

---

## AI classification + duty-rate breakdown subsystem

The duty-rate dialog in the RFQ grid uses an AI model only for HTS classification — every rate component comes from an authoritative lookup. The dialog presents the rate as a three-field breakdown (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and stores it on the row as an equation alongside the evaluated total.

```
Editor (admin / sourcing_manager)
  ├── AI Settings        Model picker (GET /api/openrouter/models, PUT /api/admin/app-settings/ai-model)
  ├── Tariffs            Section 301 CSV upload (POST /api/admin/section301/upload-csv)
  │                      Section 301 overrides (app_settings.section301_overrides)
  ├── Trump 2025 Tariffs Per-country reciprocal-rate table (GET/PUT /api/admin/trump2025/list)
  └── Factory Countries  Per-factory factory_country editor (GET /api/admin/factories, PATCH /api/admin/factories/:id)

RFQ duty-rate dialog (sourcing_manager, admin)
  ├── desc cleaner       Strips brand/size/color (src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/desc-cleaner.ts)
  ├── CROSS search       GET /api/cross/search?term= → CBP binding rulings
  ├── AI chat            POST /api/openrouter/chat → classification JSON only (no rate)
  ├── MFN lookup         GET /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=    → bundled USITC schedule
  ├── Section 301 lookup GET /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=   → override first, then parsed USTR CSV/PDF data
  └── Trump 2025 lookup  GET /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country={fobCountry}

RFQ sub-grid (per-quote)
  └── FOB Country auto-fill on Vendor pick (computeFobAutofill helper)
      ← GET /api/admin/factories/countries → { factory_nickname: factory_country }
```

**Key services:**
- `AiSettingsService` — every admin API in this subsystem (OpenRouter models, Section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, Factories).
- `HtsClassifierService` — system prompt, classification chat helpers, response parsing.
- `CrossService` — CBP CROSS proxy.

**Intentional design decisions:**
- **AI does not return rates.** The system prompt instructs the model to always return `suggested_duty_pct: null`. The MFN base rate comes from a bundled USITC HTS JSON; Section 301 comes from admin overrides first, then the uploaded USTR CSV/PDF lookup; Trump 2025 from the admin list. The model picks the HTS code; the rate lookups handle the numbers.
- **Section 301 CSV is the preferred import.** The legacy PDF endpoint still exists but is UI-hidden because regex parsing over `pdf-parse` text can mis-assign list membership when extracted text is out of order.
- **MFN rates are bundled, not live.** `data/hts-rates.json` is generated once a year by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` from `hts.usitc.gov`. O(1) lookup with 10→8→6-digit prefix fallback because the AI usually returns a 10-digit code while the rate sits on the 8-digit parent.
- **Trump 2025 rates are in `app_settings`, not bundled.** They change on political timelines, so admins maintain them through the editor UI. Lookup uses an alias table (`"PRC"`/`"Mainland China"`/`"Peoples Republic of China"` → `"China"`) so admins can use the canonical name without breaking incoming FOB-country strings.
- **Duty rate is stored as both an equation and an evaluated total.** The cell displays `40.3%`; on edit, `mathExprCellEditor` restores the `"5.3+25+10"` equation. Persisted as `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (DB) + ephemeral `[colField]_equation` (editor slot). See AGENTS.md §11 for the dual-storage rationale.
- **FOB Country auto-fill never overwrites a user value.** The sub-grid renderer pre-fills `FOB Country` from the chosen factory's `factory_country` *only* when the cell is currently empty. The rule is a pure function (`fob-country-autofill.ts`) so it can be unit-tested without AG Grid.
- **`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`** is managed via GCP Secret Manager and injected via `--set-secrets` in `cloudbuild.yaml`. Not in `--set-env-vars` (which is a full replacement on each deploy).

See [`docs/ai-classification.md`](ai-classification.md) for the full architecture including the multi-model routing pattern and prompt caching strategy.

---

## Notification system

In-app notifications are stored in the backend `user_notification` table and surfaced through a polling-based architecture.

**`NotificationService`** (`src/app/helpers/services/notification.service.ts`):
- Polls `GET /api/notifications/count` on a 30-second `interval()` Observable
- Exposes `unreadCount$` as a `BehaviorSubject<number>` consumed by the toolbar badge
- `startPolling()` / `stopPolling()` — called from `ToolbarComponent.ngOnInit()`
- CRUD wrappers: `markAsRead(id)`, `markAllAsRead()`, `getMentionableUsers()`, `createNotification(payload)`

**Toolbar badge:** The bell icon shows a red pill (capped at `99+`) when `unreadCount$ > 0`.

**Notifications page** (`src/app/pages/notifications/`): Two tabs — **Alerts** (rows from `user_notification` with mark-as-read and mark-all buttons) and **Licensing** (existing licensing notifications). Navigated to from the toolbar bell click.

**@mention autocomplete** (`src/app/pages/comments/mention-picker/`): The comment form parses `@` keystrokes and opens a user picker populated from `GET /api/notifications/mentionable-users`. Selecting a user inserts `@FirstName` into the comment text. On save, the backend (@mention pipeline in `helpers/mention.js`) triggers notifications for all mentioned users automatically.

---

## Admin impersonation

Admins can view the app exactly as any other user — same data, same role restrictions — without needing that user's credentials.

**How it works:**

1. Admin clicks **Impersonate** in Admin → Users for a normal user row, or in Admin → Users → **Factories & Reps** for a visible factory row.
2. `ImpersonationService.startImpersonation(userId, targetType?)` calls `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`; visible factory rows pass `{ targetType: 'factory' }` so the backend resolves `Factory.id`. This is the primary factory impersonation path because the production grid may not expose master-detail expand controls.
3. Nested rep rows can pass `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` if the detail grid is shown, but the factory-row button must work without those rows.
4. Backend issues a 2-hour RS256 JWT signed as the target user or vendor/factory context, with an extra `impersonated_by` claim carrying the admin's `user_id` for audit. Factory-row vendor tokens can have `email: null`; code should use `factory_id` / `role` instead of assuming a vendor email exists.
5. Frontend saves the admin's current token as `admin_token` in `localStorage` and replaces `token` with the impersonation token
6. `UserAuthInterceptor` picks up the new token automatically — no interceptor changes needed
7. All subsequent requests run as the impersonated user (backend reads identity from the JWT `Authorization` header)

**ImpersonationService** (`src/app/helpers/services/impersonation.service.ts`):
- `isImpersonating$` — `BehaviorSubject<boolean>` (true when `admin_token` is present in localStorage)
- `startImpersonation(userId, targetType?)` — calls backend, swaps tokens, hard-navigates to `/`
- `stopImpersonation()` — restores `admin_token` → `token`, clears `admin_token`, hard-navigates to `/`
- `getImpersonatedUser()` — decodes current token to return `{ name, role }` for the banner

**Impersonation banner:** An orange bar rendered at the top of `ToolbarComponent` whenever `isImpersonating$` is true: "Viewing as **[Name]** ([role]) — Stop Impersonating". The Stop button calls `stopImpersonation()`.

**Entry points:**
- Top-right name dropdown: admins see an **Impersonate** shortcut that routes to `/apps/admin/users`.
- Manage Users → **User** view: pinned-left **Impersonate** button column on normal user rows.
- Manage Users → **Factories & Reps** view: visible factory rows show pinned-left **Impersonate** buttons and use the factory target path. Expanded vendor/detail rows can also show buttons when AG Grid exposes them, but they are not required for factory impersonation.

The route is admin-only via `RoleGuardService` and `data.role: ['admin']`; no additional role check is needed in `manage_users.component.ts`.

**Intentional:** `stopImpersonation()` uses `window.location.href = '/'` rather than `router.navigate` to force a full page reload. This clears all in-memory state loaded under the impersonated identity, avoiding stale data in open components.

---

## File storage (DigitalOcean Spaces)

Item images, customer logos, and tech link files are stored in DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible). The browser uploads directly to Spaces — the backend is never in the file-transfer path.

**Upload flow (customer logos — `customer.service.ts`):**

```
1. POST /getPreSignedUrl { fileName, contentType }
      → backend returns signed PUT URL (15 min expiry, no ACL in signature)

2. Browser PUTs file to signed URL with Content-Type header
      → object created in Spaces (private by default)

3. POST /setObjectPublic { fileName }
      → backend calls putObjectAcl({ ACL: 'public-read' }) via direct authenticated API call
      → returns { publicUrl } from DO_PUBLIC_URL backend env var

4. PATCH /api/customers/:id { customers_logo: publicUrl }
      → URL saved to DB
```

**Intentional:** ACL is set in a separate `putObjectAcl` call after upload, not via the presigned URL. DigitalOcean Spaces silently ignores `x-amz-acl` headers on presigned PUTs — the object would upload successfully but stay private.

**Older item/file uploads** (`image-upload.component.ts`, `file-upload-dialog.component.ts`, `upload-file.component.ts`) construct the public URL in the frontend from `configService.DIGITAL_OCEAN_PUBLIC_URL` (`assets/config.json`) and call `POST /saveTechLink/:id` separately. These use a different code path and still rely on the bucket having the correct public access policy.

**No-image placeholders:** `NO_IMAGE_URL` and `NO_IMAGE_THUMBNAIL_URL` in `assets/config.json` point to CDN-served placeholder images and are used when an item has no uploaded image.

---

## Key design constraints

1. **No SSR** — the app is a pure SPA served as static files by nginx. All routing is client-side; nginx's `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html` handles direct URL access.
2. **No runtime env injection** — environment config (API URLs) is baked into the Angular bundle at build time. Changing the API target requires a rebuild.
3. **AG Grid Enterprise** — the license is bundled; `AllEnterpriseModule` is registered globally in `app.module.ts`.
4. **JWT in localStorage** — the token is not httpOnly; XSS mitigation relies on Angular's built-in template sanitization and strict CSP (configured in nginx).

---

## Cross-repo system map

The frontend is one part of the larger Designflow PLM runtime. Its backend
traffic goes through `designflow-bff`, which fans out to `designflow-backend`,
`designflow-item-master`, `designflow-tracking`, and
`designflow-data-syncing`. See [`system-interconnectivity.md`](system-interconnectivity.md)
for the frontend-specific interconnectivity map and
[`system-architecture.md`](system-architecture.md) for the full
multi-repo architecture.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-tracking\AGENTS.md
@@
-| Sample | `sample_id_pk` | `models/db/sample.js` | origin, direction, status, office_location, qty, retail_price, fob_cost; links `item_id_fk`→itemHeader, `prod_order_no_fk`→ProdOrderHeader |
+| Sample | `sample_id_pk` | `models/db/sample.js` | origin, direction, status, office_location, qty, retail_price, fob_cost; links `item_id_fk`→itemHeader, `prod_order_no_fk`→ProdOrderHeader, `customer_id_fk`→existing core `customers.customers_id`; courier tracking fields are `courier` + `tracking_number` |
@@
-### snake_case columns only in raw DDL
+### Quote mixed-case columns in raw DDL
 
 Looks like: other tables use mixed-case column names.
 
-Actually: all sample tables + the `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` block use snake_case.
+Actually: new sample-tracking columns should prefer snake_case, but legacy Sequelize fields such
+as `"sample_createdTime"` / `"sample_createdUser"` are mixed-case and must be quoted when added
+from raw DDL.
 
 Why: unquoted mixed-case identifiers fold to lowercase in Postgres DDL and orphan the column.
 
-Do not change because: a mixed-case `ADD COLUMN` would silently create the wrong column.
+Do not change because: an unquoted mixed-case `ADD COLUMN sample_createdTime` silently creates
+`sample_createdtime`, while Sequelize reads `"sample_createdTime"`. Guarded by
+`tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
+
+### Sample customer shipments use existing customer data
+
+What changed:
+Customer-origin samples are represented by `origin='customer'`, `direction='customer'`, and
+`customer_id_fk` on `sample`. There is also a convenience `POST /sample/customer` endpoint.
+
+Why:
+These samples originate in Ningbo and ship directly to a customer, which is neither the
+factory-to-NYC inbound path nor the USA-bought NYC-to-factory outbound path.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not create a new customer table in tracking. Use the existing core `customers` table
+(`customers.customers_id`) and keep the frontend selector backed by the core `/findCustomers`
+API.
+
+### `getUserInfo` must return decoded JWTs without callback mode
+
+What changed:
+`helpers/auth.js#getUserInfo()` uses synchronous `jwt.verify(token, publicKey, verifyOptions)`
+inside a try/catch. It must not call `jwt.verify(..., callback)` and then `await` it.
+
+Why:
+`jsonwebtoken.verify` with a callback returns `undefined`; awaiting that value loses the decoded
+user/factory context even after `authRole()` accepted the token. This breaks audit fields and
+vendor scoping.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep `getUserInfo()` covered by `tests/unit/auth.helper.test.js` when changing auth helpers.
*** End Patch

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-tracking\docs\architecture.md
@@
 **Lifecycle / state machine** (`helpers/sampleStatus.js`): a sample has an `origin`
 (factory_request / factory_offer / usa_bought / customer) that sets its `direction`:
@@
 `recordEvent` derives the next status from `(direction, current status, office, event_type)`,
 writes it under an optimistic guard (`WHERE status=<from_status>`, 0 rows → 409), and appends a
 `sample_event` row. Boxes carry their own status independent of member samples; adding a
 direction-mismatched sample to a box is skipped and reported (warn, not block).
+
+### Customer-Origin Samples
+
+What changed:
+Customer-origin samples use `origin='customer'` and `direction='customer'`. They start at
+`ningbo_checked_in`, then move through `ningbo_checked_out` to `sent_to_customer`. The normal
+`POST /sample` endpoint accepts `origin: 'customer'`, and `POST /sample/customer` is a
+convenience endpoint that forces that origin and defaults `office_location`, `next_stop`, and
+`final_destination` to the Ningbo-to-customer path.
+
+Why:
+These are physical samples sent directly from the Ningbo office to a customer, so they do not
+fit the existing factory-to-NYC or NYC-to-factory lifecycles.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep customer selection linked to the existing core `customers` table via `sample.customer_id_fk`
+(`customers.customers_id`). Do not add a duplicate customer table to tracking.
 
 **Grouping**: `sample.factory_group_id_fk` (factory shipment group) and `sample.box_id_fk`
 (current box cache) plus `sample_shipment_item` for per-leg box membership (a box can bundle
 samples from multiple factory groups).
@@
 **Integration links**: `sample.item_id_fk` (→ `itemHeader`) and `sample.prod_order_no_fk`
 (→ `ProdOrderHeader`) let licensing/production attach samples to their records.
+
+**Customer / courier links**: `sample.customer_id_fk` points at the existing core
+`customers.customers_id`. `sample.courier` is restricted to `ups`, `fedex`, `aae`, or `bombino`;
+`sample.tracking_number` stores the carrier tracking number for the current shipment.
*** End Patch

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-tracking\docs\api-reference.md
@@
 | `GET` | `/sample/:id` | Read | Get sample by id |
 | `POST` | `/sample` | Create | Create sample |
+| `POST` | `/sample/customer` | Write | Create a Ningbo-to-customer sample (`origin='customer'`) |

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md
@@
-### Sample Tracking (`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/`)
-
-Tracks physical product samples moving between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices
-(request / factory-offer / USA-bought origins; bidirectional check-in/out). A reskin over the
-`designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API using the same building blocks as the other
-tracking grids: master grid with a master-detail event timeline, plus dialogs for create,
-check-in/out, sample detail (Info/Photos/Comments/Timeline), and box/group management. Photos
-upload to DigitalOcean Spaces via a presigned-URL flow (`fetch` PUT bypasses the HTTP
-interceptors, matching the existing pattern in `helper.service.ts`). The status state machine is
-mirrored client-side in `helpers/sample.status.ts` for labels and next-status preview, but the
-server is authoritative (`POST /sample/events`). Samples live in their own backend tables (not
-`itemHeader`) and link back to licensing/production via `item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk`.
-User-facing labels avoid raw "inbound/outbound": a sample's direction shows as a **Journey**
-(`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory`) and boxes are created/shown by **route**
-(`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`); the grid has a **Box** column (from the list endpoint's
-derived `box_label`). To box samples: tick rows in the grid → **Boxes & Groups** → **Add selected**.
+### Sample Tracking (`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/`)
+
+Tracks physical product samples moving between factories, the Ningbo/NYC offices, and direct
+Ningbo-to-customer shipments. A reskin over the `designflow-tracking`
+`/api/tracking/sample` API using the same building blocks as the other tracking grids: master
+grid with a master-detail event timeline, plus dialogs for create, check-in/out, sample detail
+(Info/Photos/Comments/Timeline), and box/group management. Photos upload to DigitalOcean Spaces
+via a presigned-URL flow (`fetch` PUT bypasses the HTTP interceptors, matching the existing
+pattern in `helper.service.ts`). The status state machine is mirrored client-side in
+`helpers/sample.status.ts` for labels and next-status preview, but the server is authoritative
+(`POST /sample/events`). Samples live in their own backend tables (not `itemHeader`) and link
+back to licensing/production via `item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk`.
+
+Customer-origin samples use `origin='customer'`, `direction='customer'`, and
+`customer_id_fk` on the sample row. The create dialog loads the existing core customer list
+through `itemService.getCustomers()` (`/findCustomers`) and shows it with Material
+autocomplete; do not create or mirror a customer table in tracking. Customer shipments also store
+`courier` (`ups`, `fedex`, `aae`, `bombino`) and `tracking_number`.
+
+User-facing labels avoid raw "inbound/outbound": a sample's direction shows as a **Journey**
+(`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory` / `Ningbo → Customer`) and boxes are created/shown by
+**route** (`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`); the grid has a **Box** column (from the list
+endpoint's derived `box_label`). To box samples: tick rows in the grid → **Boxes & Groups** →
+**Add selected**.
+
+### Sample Tracking Carrier Links
+
+What changed:
+The Sample Tracking grid renders `tracking_number` as a link using `TrackingLinkRendererComponent`.
+UPS and FedEx links include the tracking number directly. Bombino supports prefilled official
+URLs via `tracking.php?searchselected=1&tracking_no=<number>`.
+
+Why:
+Bombino's official `track.js` reads `searchselected` and `tracking_no` from the URL, fills the
+tracking input, and runs the lookup. AAE's official page is an ASP.NET postback form with
+`__VIEWSTATE` / `__EVENTVALIDATION` and does not read a query parameter into its tracking
+textarea.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not invent an AAE prefill URL unless the live AAE page adds and documents one. Keep AAE
+links on `https://www.aae-global.com/tracking.aspx`; verify courier URL changes in
+`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/renderers/sample-cell-renderers.ts`.
*** End Patch

| Vendor quote response state | `req_status` (number) on each `Vendors` element | Backend; set client-side too | `null` = no response yet, `2` = responded (set when a `Price` is entered), `1` = factory **declined/passed** to quote (set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`). Drives detail-grid row class rules (`empty`/`responded`/`passed`) and the detail-grid default price sort (declined rows sink to the bottom — see §11). |
| Re-quote (stale price) flag | `requote_requested` (boolean) on each `Vendors` element | Backend `RFQVendor.requote_requested`; set client-side too | `true` when Sourcing has asked the factory to refresh a stale price. Set via the "Request new quote" detail-grid context menu (internal roles only → `itemService.requestRequote`); cleared when the vendor enters a new price or clicks the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`itemService.keepPrice`). Drives the orange price-cell highlight (`requote-requested-cell` cellClassRules, applied to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches — see §11 colId duality) and the bilingual `报价 - Quoted <date>` `tooltipValueGetter` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. |
| AG Grid column | `colId` (lowercase_underscore) | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | Stable identifier. `field` is the human-readable/storage name (can contain spaces). Never swap them — they serve different purposes. |
| JWT user payload | `role` (string), `userId` | JWT token in localStorage | Decoded by `role-guard.service.ts` using `jwt-decode`. |
| Impersonation token | `impersonated_by`, plus target `role` / `user_id` / `factory_id` for vendors | Backend `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` | Frontend stores the original admin token as `admin_token`, replaces `token`, and restores it on Stop. Visible factory rows pass `targetType: 'factory'` with `Factory.id`; rep rows pass `targetType: 'vendor'` with `vendor.vendor_id`; explicit target types avoid numeric ID collisions with normal users. Factory-row tokens may have `email: null`, so frontend code must not assume an impersonated vendor has an email. |
| Duty Rate | Object `{ title: string }` or raw number | Backend | Can arrive as an object with a `.title` property OR as a raw numeric string. Always access as `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw`. `title` holds the evaluated total; the equation breakdown lives in `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (see §11). |
| Duty rate equation | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (VARCHAR 500) | Backend `RFQItem` table | Raw 3-term sum (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`) that breaks the duty rate into MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025. Mirror lives in the row at `[duty_rate field]_equation` for `mathExprCellEditor` consumption. |
| Factory country | `Factory.factory_country` (VARCHAR 255) | Backend `Factory` table | Country where the factory operates. Drives sub-grid FOB Country auto-fill and the Trump 2025 lookup. Backfilled from the most common `vendor_country` linked to each factory at first deploy of the column. |
| CBM/pc | Field name: `"CBM/pc"`, colId: `"cbm_pc"` | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | The field name contains a slash — always retrieve via `getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')`. |
| Landed Cost | `landed_cost` colId / `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"` field | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | Computed value; formula in `calculateLandedCost()` in `rfq.component.ts`. |
| Price-for-Sales snapshot | `price_sales_snapshots` (JSON map keyed by `*_price_sales` colId) | Backend `RFQItem.rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT) | Per-row snapshot of `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}` captured the moment one of the 8 "Price for Sales" cells is populated; rendered as that cell's hover tooltip. Capture logic in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`; mirrors the `requested_price_cells` JSON-in-TEXT pattern. |
| Sample | `sample_id_pk` (number) | Backend `designflow-tracking` (`sample` table); read via `TrackingService.getSamples` | AG Grid `getRowId`. Fields: origin/direction/status/office_location/quantity/retail_price/fob_cost plus customer shipment fields `customer_id_fk`, `courier`, `tracking_number`; list endpoint also returns derived `primary_photo` and `box_label`, and the frontend decorates rows with `customer_name` from core `/findCustomers`. Status moves go through `POST /sample/events` (never edit `status` directly). Lives in its own backend tables — NOT `itemHeader`. **UI labels:** `direction` is shown as a "Journey" (`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory` / `Ningbo → Customer`) and boxes by route (`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`), via `pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts` (`directionLabel`/`boxRouteLabel`) — not raw "inbound/outbound". Frontend status mirror also in that file. |

---

## 9. Container inventory

| Container | Purpose | Managed by | Image |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frontend app | Serves Angular SPA via nginx with brotli | Google Cloud Run | Built by `Dockerfile`; pushed to Google Artifact Registry. Tag: `{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{REPO}/{ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}` |

The backend API is a separate service and a separate repository — not documented here.

+### Sample Tracking Customer and Carrier Quirks
+
+What changed:
+Customer-origin sample creation uses the existing core customer list (`itemService.getCustomers()`
+/ `/findCustomers`) with Material autocomplete and stores only `sample.customer_id_fk`. Courier
+tracking links are built in `sample-cell-renderers.ts`.
+
+Why:
+Tracking must not own a duplicate customer table. Carrier sites differ: UPS/FedEx support direct
+number URLs, Bombino supports `tracking.php?searchselected=1&tracking_no=<number>`, and AAE's
+ASP.NET tracking page does not support a reliable query-param prefill.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not add a tracking-local customer table and do not invent an AAE prefill URL unless the live
+AAE page adds a documented one. See `docs/architecture.md` Sample Tracking Carrier Links.
+
 ---
 
 ## 9. Container inventory

- If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.

## What To Update

- `AGENTS.md`: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.
- `HANDOFF.md`: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.
- Topic docs under `docs/`: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.

D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:492: | JWT token | User auth | `localStorage` at runtime | runtime only | runtime only |
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:614: 3. Each of these was a separate commit→push→wait-for-CI cycle costing ~3 min and tokens each.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:689: **Root cause:** The production nginx image was built from a commit predating the MSAL static assets. `GET /assets/auth-redirect.html` returned 404. Without this file, the OAuth popup has no bridge script to call `broadcastResponseToMainFrame()`, so the parent window never receives the token.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:699: **What happened:** `POST /api/core/auth/azure` returned 401 on `alsand.designflow.app` (and would have failed on sandbox and production too). Cloud Run logged: `The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.`
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:701: **Root cause:** The BFF uses `CORE_BACKEND_URL` as both the proxy target and the OIDC token audience when calling backends. `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to the custom domain (`https://api.core.alsand.designflow.app`). Cloud Run validates the OIDC token's `aud` claim against the service's `.run.app` URL. The custom domain does not work as an OIDC audience even though it correctly routes traffic via a Cloud Run domain mapping.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:797: | done | Sample photo upload backend wiring | `designflow-tracking` `cloudbuild.yaml` now provisions `DO_*` (Secret Manager keys + defaulted bucket/endpoint); verified live on `popcre-albert-tracking-sandbox`. |
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\AGENTS.md:834: | done | Per-turn cost display + session total | `f17ed601` — `computeTurnCost()` from per-token pricing; `sessionCostUsd` accumulates; cost in usage line and AI hint paragraph |
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:129: **Password login:**
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:130: 1. User submits email + password → `POST /auth/login` → backend returns JWT
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:131: 2. `auth.service.ts` stores token in `localStorage`
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:136: 3. MSAL returns the Azure AD ID token → `POST /auth/azure` → backend verifies the token via Azure JWKS, looks up or auto-provisions the user, returns a Designflow JWT
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:140: 1. `UserAuthInterceptor` injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every outgoing request
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:141: 2. `AuthGuardService.canActivate()` calls `auth.service.isAuthenticated()` → verifies token with backend + checks JWT expiry
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:160: **Single-use token:** `/singleUseToken/:token/:rfqItem_priceCode/:highlight` — allows vendors to access a specific RFQ item without a full account.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:162: **Token storage:** `localStorage` (not a cookie). Tokens survive page refresh and are cleared on `localStorage.clear()`.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:300: - **`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`** is managed via GCP Secret Manager and injected via `--set-secrets` in `cloudbuild.yaml`. Not in `--set-env-vars` (which is a full replacement on each deploy).
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:333: 4. Backend issues a 2-hour RS256 JWT signed as the target user or vendor/factory context, with an extra `impersonated_by` claim carrying the admin's `user_id` for audit. Factory-row vendor tokens can have `email: null`; code should use `factory_id` / `role` instead of assuming a vendor email exists.
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:334: 5. Frontend saves the admin's current token as `admin_token` in `localStorage` and replaces `token` with the impersonation token
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:335: 6. `UserAuthInterceptor` picks up the new token automatically — no interceptor changes needed
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:339: - `isImpersonating$` — `BehaviorSubject<boolean>` (true when `admin_token` is present in localStorage)
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:340: - `startImpersonation(userId, targetType?)` — calls backend, swaps tokens, hard-navigates to `/`
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:341: - `stopImpersonation()` — restores `admin_token` → `token`, clears `admin_token`, hard-navigates to `/`
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:342: - `getImpersonatedUser()` — decodes current token to return `{ name, role }` for the banner
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\architecture.md:391: 4. **JWT in localStorage** — the token is not httpOnly; XSS mitigation relies on Angular's built-in template sanitization and strict CSP (configured in nginx).
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\feature-dependency-matrix.md:14: | Authentication: password / legacy account flows | `pages/auth`, `MainService`, auth guards/interceptors | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/signup`, `/verifyAuthToken`, `/forgotPassw`, `/changePassword`, `/findUserLoginInfo` | `users`, `vendor`, auth token tables | Brevo for email flows |
D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\docs\feature-dependency-matrix.md:22: | RFQ quote requests and re-quotes | RFQ grid/detail, vendor flows | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/sendPriceReq`, `/sendMultiplePriceReq`, `/requestRequote`, `/keepPrice` | `RFQVendor`, `quote_auth_token`, `user_notification` | Brevo |

payload.message:
## Documentation Updates
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `FEATURE_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX.md` | Updated Sample tracking dependencies for customer-origin shipments, customer lookup, courier fields, and `/sample/customer`. |
| `designflow-tracking/AGENTS.md` | Added durable warnings for customer-origin samples, mixed-case audit DDL, and `getUserInfo()` JWT verification behavior. |
| `designflow-tracking/docs/api-reference.md` | Documented `/sample/customer` and new sample fields: `customer_id_fk`, `courier`, `tracking_number`. |
| `designflow-tracking/docs/architecture.md` | Added Customer-origin sample flow and existing customer-table linkage. |
| `designflow-tracking/docs/feature-dependency-matrix.md` | Updated Sample tracking row with customer autocomplete, courier fields, and backend/core dependencies. |
| `designflow-frontend/AGENTS.md` | Added sample tracking UI quirks: existing customer autocomplete, courier tracking links, and AAE prefill warning. |
| `designflow-frontend/docs/architecture.md` | Documented frontend customer shipment flow and carrier tracking URL behavior. |
| `designflow-frontend/docs/feature-dependency-matrix.md` | Updated Sample tracking row with customer lookup and courier/tracking dependencies. |

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: present in `designflow-frontend`; absent in `designflow-tracking`
- Reason: existing frontend handoff was not created or modified for this work. No new unfinished sample-tracking handoff was needed.

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: yes
- Secrets added: no
- Unknowns remaining: AAE official tracking still has no verified normal query-param prefill path; docs now warn future sessions not to invent one unless the live page documents it.

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

content.text:
Committed and pushed `sandbox-albert` in both repos.

Backend:
- Commit: `4fc957f` `Add customer sample tracking support`
- PR: [popcre/designflow-tracking#17](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-tracking/pull/17)
- Note: this PR already existed for `sandbox-albert` → `develop`, so GitHub would not allow a second PR with the same branch/base. I pushed the new commit to the existing PR.

Frontend:
